TextENGNew: ...I was dreaming.
In a six-tatami room bathed in the late afternoon sun, I lay sprawled out with a can of beer in hand, drinking since noon.
changeReason: New translation adds the specific 'six-tatami room' detail and 'drinking since noon' from the Japanese, which the original omitted, providing more concrete scene-setting.

TextENGNew: Scattered across the tatami were empty bags of potato chips and a few magazines. The open laptop's screen displayed a word processor — blank page, nothing written.
Not a single work request in over a month. Every story pitch I'd brought in had been shot down. ...I was rotting.
changeReason: Original externalizes '腐りきっていた' as 'rotten situation' while new translation correctly internalizes it as 'I was rotting,' matching the Japanese self-directed disgust.

TextENGNew: Through the wide-open window came the drone of brown cicadas, doubling the sweltering heat.
And then... the grating ring of the telephone dragged me from my drowsy haze back to reality.
Should I answer? What should I do...?
I took a sip of the beer at hand to wet my lips. Lukewarm and disgusting.
Thinking it might be a work request, I reluctantly picked up the receiver — and heard the raspy voice of an old woman.
"Are you... Ryuunosuke?"
changeReason: Original 'Is this... Ryuunosuke?' vs new 'Are you... Ryuunosuke?' -- the Japanese 'あんたが' (you) directly addresses the person, making 'Are you' more accurate.

TextENGNew: Ah... I was dreaming about that summer.
It was late summer, Heisei 15 — 2003...
changeReason: New translation preserves the Japanese calendar reference 'Heisei 15' (平成１５年) alongside 2003, which the original omitted entirely.

TextENGNew: "I am your father's mother."
The abrupt self-introduction left me speechless.
My father's mother? In other words...
"In other words, I'm your grandmother.@k@vS10/00/VT3A_araB1004. We were surprised, because we didn't know he had children."
"I... I'm surprised too.@k@vS10/62/410101105. So, what is it?!@k@vS10/62/410101106. I never even knew my father, and now you want something from me?!"
"I'm not asking for anything.@k@vS10/00/VT3A_araB1006. It's a long story, but your father died a long time ago."
"He's dead...? Seriously?!"

"Eight years ago, he had an accident on a fishing boat.@k@vS10/00/VT3A_araB1008. And after sorting through his belongings, we found a diary and some tape recordings he made at work.@k@vS10/00/VT3A_araB1009. He mentioned you in his writings."
It came so suddenly, I struggled to understand it. But it did add up.
Since I had plenty of free time at the moment, I hopped on the train that same day and headed to Osaka, where my grandparents lived.
The elderly couple lived in an apartment in Osaka prefecture.
Based on my father's diary and the stories my grandparents told me, my father was a journalist working for a weekly photo magazine in Tokyo.
But he suffered a mental breakdown after a certain scoop, and after numerous transfers, ultimately retired from his position as a reporter.
He was acting strange at the time, and returned to Osaka where his parents lived in order to escape from everything.
He also cut off all communication with the woman he was dating at the time... in other words, my mother.
He didn't do much work after returning to Osaka. He dug into his savings while scraping by on a day to day basis.
...And one day, eight years ago, he drowned in an accident where his fishing boat capsized.
My father's life came to an end before he was able to recover from the hardships he experienced in Tokyo...
"So, he had no inheritance left to leave to you.@k@vS10/00/VT3A_araB1011. Sorry."
"That's fine.@k@vS10/62/410101109. I wasn't expecting anything to begin with."
I've never met any of my father's relatives, so they were essentially strangers to me. I didn't have any strong feelings when I met them.
My grandfather's eyes looked somewhat similar to mine... but that was it.
And based on the dispassionate way they looked at me, they probably felt the same about me.
They probably would've felt more empathetic if I was a child, but I was already a full-grown adult. ...That was the feeling I got from their expressions.
"Anyway... Back in Tokyo, he was investigating a village called Hinamizawa."
"Hinamizawa?@k@vS10/62/410101111. The site of the Great Hinamizawa Disaster?!"
"Yeah.@k@vS10/00/VT3A_araB1014. We found some tape recordings of his interviews with the survivors from the village..."
"We had a detective agency do some research to find you, and the detective mentioned that you were a reporter in Tokyo, so we thought it might be useful to you... Take a look."
As she said that, my grandmother handed me two cassette tapes.
The labels were faded and peeling off, but I could just barely read the titles "November 24th 1983, Shion Sonozaki" and "November 28th 1983, Keiichi Maebara."
According to my grandfather, these were recordings of interviews with survivors of the Great Hinamizawa Disaster of 1983.
The tapes were in poor condition and likely to break at any time... but I decided to take them home anyway, just in case they sparked a story of some sort.
...The fact that these belonged to my father meant little to me.
On the way back, I stopped by a second-hand shop and bought a cassette player they were about to throw away, then returned to my tiny apartment.
...I'd heard various rumors and theories about the Great Hinamizawa Disaster in the past.
Which means I might be able to write an occult article based on these tapes and sell it somewhere.
With high expectations, I slipped the "Keiichi Maebara" tape into the cassette player and pressed the play button.
Unfortunately, no matter which way I rotated the tape, I couldn't hear anything but harsh static and faint unintelligible sounds.
I expected better results from something produced with such expensive equipment. And what's more, I didn't even get a return on my own investment here.
(Well... the tape is over 20 years old, after all.)
Feeling slightly disappointed, I decided I might as well grab the "Shion Sonozaki" tape and try playing it.
Despite my low expectations... I suddenly heard a voice breaking through the silence.
Judging by the tone and intonation, she was female, likely a young girl. ...Perhaps she's the "Shion Sonozaki" from the label on the tape.
"...In the end... was exactly right.@k@vS10/06/ds_shi002. Oyashiro-sama really does exist... and the curse is real.@k@vS10/06/ds_shi003. And that curse is what killed everyone..."
Oyashiro-sama...? And... the curse?
There was a haggard tone in her voice as she muttered that.
I quickly stopped the tape and carefully rewound it, taking care not to break it. Then I put on my headphones and started listening from the beginning.
"...In the end, what she said was exactly right.@k@vS10/06/ds_shi005. Oyashiro-sama really does exist... and the curse is real.@k@vS10/06/ds_shi006. And that curse is what killed everyone..."

"When that girl blew up the school, I thought she'd committed the crime out of insanity and cursed her for killing my sister.@k@vS10/06/ds_shi008. But the very next day, the gas disaster took everyone in Hinamizawa..."
"The only other survivor was Kei-chan, who was being questioned by the police at the time just like me..."
"Kei-chan"...?
I was confused for a moment, not knowing who that was... but then I looked at the label on the other tape in my hand.
She's probably referring to the "Keiichi Maebara" from this tape.
Unfortunately, I had no way to confirm this information.
(Even so...)
I felt an eerie sensation as I listened to the tape.
The idea that the Great Hinamizawa Disaster was caused by the "curse of Oyashiro-sama" is one of the least credible theories at the moment.

But... even at the time this interview was conducted, they likely doubted it. After all, even though modern Japanese people are quick to fear paranormal phenomena, they tend not to truly believe in it.
They'd say "That's impossible" or "Ridiculous" any time it came up in idle chatter.
The materialistic values of the Western world have penetrated into our society, turning our perception of spiritual or speculative phenomena upside down.

But despite insisting that everything can be explained through science... they still find themselves fascinated with supernatural phenomena, and they can't help but fear they might be "cursed" for doing so.
So, while they maintain their superficial posture of "disbelief," they're still terrified to face that horror head on... They merely turn their eyes from its existence.
But on the other hand, they have a desire to understand the things they fear, and they're willing to get dangerously close to do so. ...I think that's the true nature of people drawn to the occult.
"...Well, was the gas disaster actually caused by Oyashiro-sama's curse?@k@vS10/00/VT3A_araF1002. I think that's too heavy-handed an explanation."
I heard an arrogant voice speak up shortly after the girl finished talking.
...That was the first time I'd heard my father's voice. It lacked the depth and astringency I would've expected, but then I remembered that this was when my father was still young, and was convinced.
"...To tell the truth, I'm skeptical of the occult.@k@vS10/00/VT3A_araF1004. I haven't witnessed it with my own eyes, and this second-hand information sounds illegitimate."
"That's why I believe there must be some artificial element to this disaster.@k@vS10/00/VT3A_araF1006. I thought it would be appropriate to ask you about that."

"...Wow...@k@vS10/06/ds_shi011. That sounds a lot like how I was until recently...@k@vS10/06/ds_shi012. I didn't believe in curses, or anything like that.@k@vS10/06/ds_shi013. Until the disaster occurred...@k@vS10/06/ds_shi014. Right, Sis...?"
Just then.
It sounded like my father's voice cut out after gasping.
And then...
"...Believe or don't believe, that's your choice.@k@vS10/06/ds_shi016. But if you keep stubbornly believing in your own ridiculous preconceptions... maybe you'll be cursed too?"
"Well, if you can still run that mouth of yours even after you see it for yourself, you really are something.@k@vS10/06/ds_shi018. Ehehehe..."
...What happened?
My father's tone and demeanor completely changed... He groaned in astonishment... his fear audible through the static of the old tape.
What could've happened...?
Or rather... what on earth did he see?!
"Tomitake-san died clawing his throat out.@k@vS10/06/ds_shi020. Takano-san burned to death in the mountains.@k@vS10/06/ds_shi021. Sis, Rika-chama, and Satoko were blown to pieces."
"...So, how about you?@k@vS10/06/ds_shi023. What sort of death can you hope for...?"
"Ah, ...ahahahahah...@k@vS10/00/VT3A_araF1011. Th-that's quite an elaborate joke.@k@vS10/00/VT3A_araF1012. J-just what are you getting at...?"
"...From now on, watch out for footsteps.@k@vS10/06/ds_shi025. If you come to a sudden stop while walking down the road... and you hear one extra footstep... that's Oyashiro-sama."
"It means you're being followed. Being cursed..."
"...Aah, you might be able to hear it even now.@k@vS10/06/ds_shi028. Right here......"
"...See?@k@vS10/06/ds_shi030. You really heard it.@k@vS10/06/ds_shi031. Then it's been decided. Ehehe...!"

"Tomitake-san committed suicide,@k@vS10/06/ds_shi033. Takano-san was immolated,@k@vS10/06/ds_shi034. Sis and the others died in the explosion,@k@vS10/06/ds_shi035. and you are going to drown to deaaaath!!@k@vS10/06/ds_shi036. Keheheheheh, hyaahahahahahahah!!!"
I jumped back immediately and threw the headphones aside without even thinking.
Wh... what was that just now...?!
A-and... and even more than that!
The girl on the tape clearly said he would "drown" to death...
And my father really did drown to death... just as this prophecy foretold...?
I heard a footstep coming from behind.
Wait... Please, just wait a minute!
Wh-what is "Oyashiro-sama" doing here?!
"Ugh, uwaaaaaaah!!"
That moment, the tatami floor transformed into a dark void... If I fell into it, I'd be swallowed whole.
Then, my consciousness quickly faded...
My dream ended there... and I awakened.
I slowly opened my eyes.
The ceiling of an unfamiliar room was spread out before me.
Where... am I...?
I got up and took a look around.
It was a spacious, tatami-lined room.
Aah... I was dreaming...
"Where am I...?"
"Are you feeling all right?"
An unfamiliar man was kneeling by my side.
"Uwah?!"
"What's wrong?"
"Uh... W-who are you?"
"Who am I...? First off, who are you?"
"Ah... My name is Arakawa.@k@vS10/62/410101117. I'm a freelance writer."
I struggled to piece together my current situation.
Based on his outfit... he looks like a plainclothes officer. Probably a detective following up on Otobe's report.
"Umm... I assume you've already heard about the suicide party in the car?"
"Huh?"
"Oh... umm, you're a police officer, right...?"
"Er... Ah, yes.@k@vS10/00/VT3A_FkeijiA1006. That's true, but..."
I wasn't thinking clearly at all.
It felt like my mind was in a haze.
How can I explain this...?
I took a look around.
It was still dark outside the window.
How long was I asleep for...?
There was nobody else in the room aside from this officer and myself.
Huh...? Nobody...?
"What's wrong?"
"Umm... what about the others...?!"
"Others...?"
"Miyuki-chan...@k@vS10/62/410101122. Ah, I mean, weren't there two women and a man in this room with me...?"
"No.@k@vS10/00/VT3A_FkeijiA1010. You were the only one here when I arrived."
What is going on...?
We were all here, drinking the tea Towada-san brought us.
And then after feeling relieved, we started getting tired and fell asleep.
That much made sense.
The question was what came after.
Wondering how long I had slept for, I glanced at my watch.
The hands pointed to 11 o'clock.
That can't be...!
When I arrived in Hinamizawa in Miyuki-chan's car, it must've been around 10 or 11 PM. Which means...
"Umm... today is the 22nd of June, right?"
"Huh? Yeah, well..."
The policeman gave me a dubious look.
...For now, I asked him that to verify if the events that transpired before I fell asleep were just a dream. But that appears not to be the case.
If we assume that... then... what does this mean?
"When did you arrive here?"
"...? Just a moment ago?"
Right. There were actually four people drinking tea in here.
The paper cups that should've been sitting in front of us were all tidied up.
"Is something wrong?"
There were no indications of other people. Where did everyone go...?
Even though I looked around, it appeared I was the only one here.
"By the way, Arakawa-san.@k@vS10/00/VT3A_FkeijiA1015. You mentioned something about a suicide party...?"
"Oh, umm, yes..."
"Would you show me where?"
As I sat there absentmindedly, the policeman grabbed my hand and asked that in a monotone voice.
I gave him a vague answer, but... for some reason, alarm bells were going off inside my head.
It feels like there's a far more serious incident going on than a group suicide.
No, it's not just a feeling.
Everyone is gone, aside from myself. ...Could this be another disappearing incident?!
"Officer! Uh..."
The policeman forcibly lifted me by the arm, seeming to ignore what I was trying to say. And then,
"Please show me the way.@k@vS10/00/VT3A_FkeijiA1019. Where is the suicide car?"
"Well... more importantly...@k@vS10/62/410101134. I think there are more important things going on right here at the moment..."
"I'll listen to what you have to say while we head to the car."
The policeman tried to avoid making eye contact as he started walking off.
There's... something weird about this policeman...!
The alarm bells were ringing even louder inside my head.
"What's the matter?@k@vS10/00/VT3A_FkeijiA1022. Come this way, Arakawa-san."
I left the assembly hall, pretty much dragged away by the police officer.
The rain was still coming down hard.
Three more police officers were waiting just outside the assembly hall.
Then, as I descended the stone steps, it felt like I was surrounded by them.
A black car was parked at the base of the stone steps.
Wait a minute. Why is it... a black car?
Even if these are plainclothes detectives, shouldn't they have come in a patrol car...?
The policeman I met earlier pushed me into the backseat, then sat down next to me.
"This man... allegedly witnessed a group suicide."
"Hooh...?"
"Yes, well..."
"What was the model of the car?@k@vS10/00/VT3A_FkeijiB1003. How many people were onboard?"
"Uh... How many people..."
"You saw corpses... didn't you?"
I could hear a hint of curiosity in the police officer's voice.
I shot a look of displeasure at the officer sitting next to me.
"I didn't get a very good look."
The policeman sounded disappointed as he said that. ...With no further questions, the car began lurching forward.

"Even though the ban was recently lifted, there aren't any people living in this area.@k@vS10/00/VT3A_FkeijiB1007. If you hadn't found that suicide party's car, it would have gone a long time before being discovered."
"I bet we'll find a bone or two if we dig around that area.@k@vS10/00/VT3A_FkeijiA1025. I don't want to come to a village like this ever again. Haha..."
The officer holding the steering wheel spoke in a tone that made it sound like we were heading off on a sightseeing trip.
The high beams lit the way as we traveled down the pitch-black road. No matter where I looked, I couldn't see anything but trees.
The officer sitting beside me pulled out a cigarette and lit it with his lighter.
The surrounding area was illuminated for a moment.
In that moment, I noticed something red glittering between the seat and backrest.
A ruby earring...?
I'm certain that... Miyuki-chan was wearing ruby earrings.
Which means... that was one of Miyuki-chan's earrings...?
But the light disappeared too quickly for me to make certain... and the car was once again enveloped in darkness.
"Oh, right... do you mind if I smoke?"
His unpleasant tone made it hard to say anything back.
In my current situation, the stench of cigarette smoke was of no consequence.
The question is... what is Miyuki-chan's earring doing in a place like this?
...There's no way her earring could've fallen off in here.
In which case, maybe it's just a coincidence...?
"Something wrong, Arakawa-san...?"
"Huh? No..."
As I sat beside the smoking police officer, I held my breath and started thinking.
...What on earth happened to Miyuki-chan?
And speaking of that, I was worried about Towada-san and Otobe, too.
No way... Were they all demoned away...?
And maybe... these guys are the ones responsible...?
Those disturbing ideas dominated my thought process.
The warning bells inside my head were ringing even louder.
I wanted to get out of here. I wanted to get out of this car and run away, as soon as possible.
"Anyway... you mentioned earlier that there was some other awful thing going on. What was that?"
"Umm... nothing..."
"Don't hold back. Please, tell me."
"Well... was there really nobody else in the assembly hall?"
"No, nobody."
The policeman stared vacantly at me.
"There was nobody else there. Just you."
"There shouldn't be anyone in this abandoned village in the first place.@k@vS10/00/VT3A_FkeijiB1011. Maybe... you saw a ghost?"
The officer completely rejected my assertion.
Eventually, we passed a fork in the road and started heading downhill.
"H-hold on... this isn't the right way?!"
"It's all right. This is the way."
"No, the suicide car isn't this way.@k@vS10/62/410101147. It was back to the left."
"A group suicide...?@k@vS10/00/VT3A_FkeijiB1013. Do you... really want to see something like that...?"
The officer said that in a menacingly low voice.
A chill crawled up my spine.
These people... They're not police officers...!
Doubts sprung up inside me.
"So... where are we headed?"
I tried my best to stay calm as I asked that.
My heart was pounding so hard it was about to leap out of my chest at any moment.
"That's... not something you need to know."
The car climbed the mountain road, then descended... We passed by the swamp where the gas originated from, then drove along the river.
"This... isn't the way to Okinomiya, is it...?"
"Hehehe...@k@vS10/00/VT3A_FkeijiB1015. You don't need to know where you're going..."
The car pressed on in the drizzling rain, weaving through the darkness...
Several months later...
Summer had ended, autumn had ended, and the new year was about to begin.
The editorial department at the "Weekly Seven" was bustling, like always. Phones were ringing, people were talking, and books and manuscripts were piling up on everyone's desks.
"Then, let's use that for the ghost special edition!"
The faces of the young editorial staff members lit up once Editor Kanou gave them the go-ahead. Among the young editorial staff... Arakawa was absent.
Kanou quickly finished up the paragraph, then grabbed a cigarette and sat down on the sofa for a much needed break.
"...If Arakawa was here, it would've been more interesting. ...Haah~."
He muttered that in a defeated tone.
Ryuunosuke Arakawa had gone missing after leaving to investigate Hinamizawa a few months ago.
There was a good chance he was caught up in some sort of incident. Though the police have been searching desperately, they still haven't found any clues.

Rumors suggest that the Sonozaki Group put pressure on the police over the course of the investigation. Naturally, the Sonozaki Group denied any involvement in the incident, and there was no evidence to substantiate those rumors.
People on the internet have been whispering that this is the work of Oyashiro-sama's curse.
Word got out that Arakawa's father had also investigated Hinamizawa in the past, so the curse claimed the lives of two generations of family.
"I wonder if it really is a curse killing people...@k@vS10/00/VT3A_desk1004. I'm sure he would think so..."
Kanou smiled bitterly, then changed the channel on the TV.
"On to our next story.@k@vS10/00/VT3A_Tvann1002. Earlier today, the skeletal remains of a body were discovered near the mouth of the Kisogawa river in Mie prefecture."
The next moment, Arakawa's face was being displayed on the TV screen. Kanou was so shocked he dropped his cigarette.

"Belongings discovered in the vicinity allowed authorities to identify the man as Ryuunosuke Arakawa-san.@k@vS10/00/VT3A_Tvann1004. The police are doing their utmost to learn more about this incident..."
"Arakawa-chan...?"
Kanou rushed back to his desk and spread out a map.
In addition to Mie prefecture, the Kisogawa river also ran through Aichi and Gifu...
Following the river upstream... led to Hinamizawa village.
"What the hell is going on...?"
"Editor.@k@vS10/00/VT3A_kishaA1002. Should I add an article about this guy to the ghost special edition?"
A young editor watching TV called out to Kanou.
"Ah, yes... Please do."
His voice sounded surprisingly curious... which was to be expected, since this man wasn't acquainted with Arakawa. As Kanou pondered that, he turned back toward the TV...
"Curse of Hinamizawa village claims the lives of father and son." ...Suddenly, titles like that ran through the back of Kanou's mind.

And what happened to Ryuunosuke Arakawa's travel companion Miyuki Sorimachi? And the people he met there, Akira Otobe, Yae Towada, Takumi Kurosawa, and Mion Sonozaki...? Their fate was completely in the dark.
The truth of this incident remains a mystery.
The truth behind all these mysteries was swallowed by the village where man-eating demons dwelled...
"I'll go with you."
"Ah, me too...!"
I threw on my sneakers and dashed out of the assembly hall after Sonozaki-san. And Otobe hastily chased after me from behind.
